html:
Start: Aligned Axes
⚠ Figure failed to load

plan.contextChunk:
consecutive frames. After a small time interval, $\Delta t$, the camera
will move, generating a displacement in the points with respect to the
camera coordinate system equal to:

$$\mathbf{P}_{t+\Delta t} = - \mathbf{T}_{\Delta t}  + \mathbf{R}_{\Delta t} \mathbf{P}_t
$$
where $\mathbf{T}_{\Delta t}$ is the camera translation and
$\mathbf{R}_{\Delta t}$ is the camera rotation that took place over that
time interval, $\Delta t$. The velocity of a 3D point with respect to
the camera will be:
$$
\dot{\mathbf{P}} = \frac{\mathbf{P}_{t+\Delta t} - \mathbf{P}_{t}}{\Delta t} = - \mathbf{V} + \frac{\mathbf{R}_{\Delta t} -\mathbf{I}}{\Delta t} \mathbf{P}_t
$${#eq-general_camera_motion_equation_full}

To derive the rotation, we consider the **Euler angles**
(@fig-yaw_pitch_roll) and decompose the rotation using rotations along
the three axes (yaw, pitch, roll):

![Rotation expressed by Euler angles (yaw, pitch, roll).](figures/optical_flow/yaw_pitch_roll.png){width="40%" #fig-yaw_pitch_roll}

Each angle measures the rotation along the camera-coordinate axes. Using
this representation of the rotation, the rotation matrix can be written
as: 
$$\begin{aligned}
\mathbf{R}_{\Delta t} = 
\begin{bmatrix}
\cos \theta_Z &  \sin \theta_Z & 0\\
-\sin \theta_Z &  \cos \theta_Z & 0\\
0 &  0 & 1
\end{bmatrix}
\begin{bmatrix}
\cos \theta_Y &  0 & -\sin \theta_Y\\
0 &  1 & 0\\
\sin \theta_Y &  0 & \cos \theta_Y

elements: 3D world coordinate axes (X, Y, Z) in black
Camera coordinate axes (x, y, z) in green overlayed on shaded gray plane
Black point at origin (0,0,0)
Point P in blue with connecting dashed gray line to origin
Point p in blue in camera frame (projected from P)
Red curved arrows for rotations (Yaw on Y, Pitch on X, Roll on Z)
Axis labels (X, Y, Z, x, y, z, P, p)
Angle notations θX, θY, θZ placed by rotation axes
Text for Yaw, Pitch, Roll with corresponding arrows
Shaded gray plane representing camera image plane

interactions.label: Yaw (θY)
interactions.teaches: Shows how rotating the scene around the Y axis (yaw) changes the orientation of the coordinate system and point P relative to the camera.

interactions.label: Pitch (θX)
interactions.teaches: Shows how rotating the scene around the X axis (pitch) tilts the camera up or down, shifting how the image plane intersects point P.

interactions.label: Roll (θZ)
interactions.teaches: Demonstrates how rolling the camera about the Z axis rotates the image plane and the position of point p within it.

demo_steps.title: Start: Aligned Axes
demo_steps.narration: Here the camera axes are perfectly aligned with the world axes. Point P is projected as p onto the image plane, and no rotations have been applied yet.

demo_steps.title: Yaw Rotation
demo_steps.narration: Now, let's rotate the scene by 45° around the Y axis (yaw). Notice how both the camera axes and point P rotate horizontally, simulating the effect of turning the camera left or right. The position of p on the image plane shifts accordingly.

demo_steps.title: Add Pitch
demo_steps.narration: Next, we pitch the camera by 30° upward about the X axis. The image plane tilts, and the projection of P onto p slides, illustrating how pitch influences where points appear in the camera view.

demo_steps.title: Full Rotation: Add Roll
demo_steps.narration: Finally, we apply a 60° roll around the Z axis. Watch as the image plane and camera axes spin around the view direction. The relative location of p in the image changes, demonstrating how all three Euler angles combine to reorient the camera and its perception of the world.

interactionPlan.camera_suggestion: Start with an isometric view slightly above and to the side, so all three axes and both coordinate systems are visible. Ensure the rotation arrows and points P and p are never occluded. Fit major labels onscreen without crowding.
interactionPlan.notes: When applying rotations, animate axes, plane, and labels together for clarity. Red arrows indicating each rotation should fade in or pulse as the corresponding angle is increased in each step. Use opacity or line style to contrast world and camera axes. Visualize projection from P to p with a pulsing dashed line.

attempts.html:
Start: Aligned Axes
⚠ Figure failed to load

discrepancies: The world axes and points P and p are faithfully represented, but the size and placement of text labels are not as bold and clear as in the original.
The red arc arrows for yaw, pitch, and roll are less visually prominent than in the reference and not as accurately placed relative to axis ends.
Green camera axes x/y/z are present but the z label and axis appear less prominent than x and y and the correspondence is less clear.
The background projection lines are present, but the dashed style and color contrast are less strong than in the source image.
Some subtle presentation/arrangement differences (e.g., spatial layout of roll label and θ_z) reduce immediate clarity.
evaluation.notes: All main axes, plane, points, and rotation arcs are present and interactive, though some visual clarity and labeling could be improved.
action_items: Increase the prominence and vividness of the red arc arrows and ensure correct placement exactly as in reference (over axes, larger, fully visible).
Bold all axis and point labels more aggressively for clarity; match font weights and sizes to the original.
Enhance dashed lines for projections to be darker and higher-contrast as in reference.
Ensure the camera axes x/y/z labeling and orientation is spatially unambiguous (e.g. larger arrows, clearer offsets).
Adjust label placement and offset for θ_z and Roll to match textbook composition.

feedback.rationale: All action items and discrepancies concern visual styling, label size, layout, and clarity rather than conceptual errors or missing elements, so improved generation is needed.
action_items: Increase the prominence and vividness of the red arc arrows and ensure correct placement exactly as in reference (over axes, larger, fully visible).
Bold all axis and point labels more aggressively for clarity; match font weights and sizes to the original.
Enhance dashed lines for projections to be darker and higher-contrast as in reference.
Ensure the camera axes x/y/z labeling and orientation is spatially unambiguous (e.g. larger arrows, clearer offsets).
Adjust label placement and offset for θ_z and Roll to match textbook composition.
actionItems: Increase the prominence and vividness of the red arc arrows and ensure correct placement exactly as in reference (over axes, larger, fully visible).
Bold all axis and point labels more aggressively for clarity; match font weights and sizes to the original.

Ensure the camera axes x/y/z labeling and orientation is spatially unambiguous (e.g. larger arrows, clearer offsets).

attempts.html:
Start: Aligned Axes
⚠ Figure failed to load

discrepancies: The camera/image plane's border is too dark and could be lighter to match the original's translucent effect.
The 3D arcs for rotation (Yaw, Pitch, Roll) are lighter/redder in the original, but somewhat pale/pink here.
The world axes arrows are black in both figures, but the tips are less prominent than the thick arrows in the source.
Projection lines and points are more emphasized in the original (thicker/dashed and with larger labels).
The right side's Z/P axis zigzag dash and its break at P are present but less visually clear than in the source.
evaluation.notes: Structure, geometry, labels, and interactions closely match the original, but minor color and thickness mismatches slightly reduce faithfulness.
action_items: Increase thickness and prominence of dashed projection lines and the main world axes arrowheads for closer visual match.
Adjust opacity and color for the Yaw, Pitch, and Roll arcs/arrows to match the vivid red in the original (instead of pale pink).
Enhance image plane border's contrast/opacity for more visual parity with the textbook figure.
Slightly enlarge label font sizes for P, p, and axis tips for better legibility.
Refine the Z/P axis zigzag/dashed connection to more clearly represent the break at P as in the source.

feedback.rationale: All action items call for visual refinements such as adjusting colors, thickness, opacity, and label size, which are issues with execution rather than conceptual planning.
action_items: Increase thickness and prominence of dashed projection lines and the main world axes arrowheads for closer visual match.
Adjust opacity and color for the Yaw, Pitch, and Roll arcs/arrows to match the vivid red in the original (instead of pale pink).

Slightly enlarge label font sizes for P, p, and axis tips for better legibility.
Refine the Z/P axis zigzag/dashed connection to more clearly represent the break at P as in the source.

attempts.html:
Start: Aligned Axes
⚠ Figure failed to load

discrepancies: Red arc arrows are transparent/pale and less bold than in the source figure
The gray dashed world-to-P and projection lines are visible but not as subtle as in the source figure
The green camera axes are thicker and less visually integrated into the image plane than in the source
The font family and size are mostly correct but minor misplacements and spacing exist on a few labels
The Z label for the world axis is smaller and less prominent than in the original
evaluation.notes: Figure is highly faithful with robust, meaningful interactions and correct concept, but a few label and color nuances are slightly off.
action_items: Bolden and increase opacity of the red arc rotation arrows for better visual match
Refine label placement and size for x, y, z, and Z axis to better match the original, especially for Z
Slightly reduce the thickness and saturation of the green camera axes for better integration
Soften the gray dashed lines to be more subtle as in the reference
Ensure that all labels are positioned identically to the source, with less crowding and more precise visual alignment

feedback.rationale: All feedback concerns minor execution details like color, line thickness, label placement, and opacity, indicating the plan is correct but generation needs refinement.
